description: A partial re-embed leaves old and new model vectors in one index, corrupting similarity.
body: Failure mode: an embedding-model upgrade re-embeds only part of the corpus in place, so the index holds a mix of old-model and new-model vectors that are not comparable, and recall degrades unpredictably. Detection signals: inconsistent recall that varies by which documents were re-embedded, and an index whose vectors trace to two different model versions. Do not continue serving the mixed index while finishing the migration. Safer fallback: build a fresh index by re-embedding the entire corpus with the new model, validate recall against the old index on the labeled set, then atomically switch queries and embedder together via a blue/green swap.
